behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all asserted paths (npm/grep/src/plugin.ts, register.ts, package.json, README.md), required literal strings (Cypress.expose(, --expose, expose:, >=15.10.0, 'Migrating from v5 to v6'), and the config keys.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what the new API should be but doesn't give exact line replacements; the agent must figure out the internals (e.g., how plugin detects expose vs env).

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check behavioral outcomes (env-only returns unchanged, expose logs grep) and literal API strings which are inherent to the migration, not gold-specific variable names.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_plugin_reads_from_expose actually imports and calls plugin() with env and expose configs and verifies runtime behavior; other tests run node subprocesses.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub plugin that returns config would fail the expose-case assertion (must log 'login'); a stub register.ts would fail the Cypress.expose( literal check.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All five behavioral requirements in instruction.md map to test functions (plugin, register, peerDep, scripts, README).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are organized under commented sections (Gates, Pass-to-pass, Fail-to-pass) with descriptive names.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data outputs.

no_typos.reason: Filenames and literals are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Files are named at module level (which is the task scope), not specific line numbers.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: package.json edits are localized to peerDependencies.cypress and specific grep-related scripts.
